economic_notes: Nation has investments in three government business enterprises: Cascade Lower Canyon Community Forest LP (33%), Smemela:lh Group Management Ltd. (100%), and Stelmexw Enterprises LP (99%). Cascade Lower Canyon Community Forest LP reported net income of $2,224,151 on revenue of $11,749,831.
notes: Administration & Education and Health & Social Development segments are reported combined in segment schedule; expenses_education includes full Administration & Education segment of $1,048,021 and expenses_health includes full Health & Social Development segment of $977,551. No remuneration schedule included in the financial statements. Economic Development & Employment segment generated the largest surplus at $2,632,175 driven largely by $3,536,594 in economic activities and other revenue. Portfolio investments of $6,179,145 were newly acquired in the year via BMO Securities Portfolio held in Yale First Nation Community Trust. Three GBEs accounted for under modified equity method. Two entities fully consolidated: Y.F.N. Developments Inc. and Yale First Nation Community Trust. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $1,373,441 + FNHA $455,046 + CMHC $47,832 + Other government $27,529 + DFO $299,264 + Province of BC $228,619 + Pacific Salmon Commission $57,656 + New Relationship Trust $3,600 + Ottawa Trust Income $3,846 = $2,496,833 plus segment other government $1,557,943 reconciliation differs; using statement of operations figures summing ISC + FNHA + CMHC + Other government + DFO + Province of BC + Pacific Salmon Commission + New Relationship Trust + Ottawa Trust = $2,496,833 as total government transfers, OSR = $7,794,326 - $2,496,833 = $5,297,493. However revenue_lines other revenue $3,552,491 + earnings from GBEs $901,902 + investment income $575,540 + rental income $267,560 = $5,297,493 as OSR. Values updated accordingly.
